meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Project Management for Students: Stop Abandoning Your AI Apps
content.seo_description: Stop abandoning your code. Master advanced project management using Antigravity and Claude Code to finish your student projects and build a professional portfolio.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of scope control, not a lack of talent. By implementing a modular, ticket-driven workflow using Antigravity for architectural planning and Claude Code for iterative execution, you break the cycle of overwhelm and ship production-ready features consistently.
content.intro:
You stare at the blinking cursor, the remnants of a half-baked SaaS idea mocking your ambition. It starts with a flicker of genius, spirals into a chaotic web of scope creep, and ends in the graveyard of abandoned GitHub repositories. You have the skills, but you lack the infrastructure to cross the finish line.

This isn't about working harder; it is about engineering your workflow. We are moving beyond manual project management into the era of AI-orchestrated development. By leveraging Antigravity for structural integrity and Claude Code for rapid implementation, you will transform your student projects into professional-grade assets.

steps.title: Define the Minimum Viable Scope
steps.description: Stop building features that don't serve the core value. Use Antigravity to map your MVP into atomic tasks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my project idea and break it down into the smallest possible functional MVP components. Exclude any features not strictly necessary for the core user flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a project roadmap file named roadmap.md based on these atomic tasks.'

steps.title: Initialize the Technical Architecture
steps.description: Establish a robust foundation. Your file structure should be optimized for AI-assisted development.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a clean, scalable folder structure for a Next.js and Supabase application. Prioritize modularity for future feature expansion.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the directory structure and initialize the base configuration files.'

steps.title: Implement Ticket-Driven Development
steps.description: Convert your roadmap into individual tickets. Never write code without a clearly defined task.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Convert the roadmap.md into a series of GitHub-style issue tickets, each with clear acceptance criteria.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Create a tickets/ directory with individual markdown files for each task.'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Execution
steps.description: Execute one ticket at a time. This prevents context switching and scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the implementation strategy for the next ticket: [Insert Ticket Name]. Provide the necessary function signatures.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the code for [Ticket Name] based on the provided signatures.'

steps.title: Automated Quality Assurance
steps.description: Before merging, use AI to audit your code against the requirements defined in your tickets.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Review the current code against the acceptance criteria in the active ticket. Identify any edge cases missed.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Run a final code review on the latest changes and suggest necessary unit tests.'

pro_tips: Treat every project like a commercial product; if a feature doesn't have a ticket, it doesn't exist.
Use Claude Code to document as you build; if you can't explain it in the README, you haven't mastered the logic.
Limit your WIP (Work In Progress) to one ticket at a time. Parallelism is the enemy of completion for solo builders.
Leverage Antigravity to generate tests before you write the logic; this forces you to think about the output before the implementation.
Schedule 'Code Hygiene' sessions once a week where you delete dead code rather than refactoring it indefinitely.
content.next_steps: Once your first project is live, deploy to Vercel or Railway and begin documenting your development journey in a public devlog to attract potential recruiters.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your brilliant ideas die in a text editor. Mastering the project management workflow is the single most important transition from student to senior developer. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
